Starmer announces extra £15bn for UK defence, with some road and energy projects scrapped to help fund it

Lilo & Stitch star Daveigh Chase's cause of death was Aids
Sport
Wimbledon day two - follow live
Arsenal poised - can Newcastle convince Guimaraes for a second time?
'I never expected to be here' - Williams returns to Wimbledon
'Germany no longer first-class team ' - is this the end for Nagelsmann?
Pick Mainoo over Anderson against DR Congo - Rooney
The BBC is in multiple languages
Read the BBC In your own language
The prime minister says the extra funding - which falls short of the £28bn the Ministry of Defence requested - will "make the British people safer".
People in areas devastated by twin earthquakes say they need more support from the government.
The blast was caused by an explosive device which appeared to contain bolts and pellets, the head of Monaco's government said.
Thousands of people have left South Africa ahead of Tuesday's deadline set by anti-migrant groups.
The case against Gojek founder Nadiem Makarim relates to a laptop deal made during his term as Indonesia's education minister.
Guo Wengui branded himself as a China critic, gaining followers who backed his fraudulent schemes.
As Malaysian durians are being sold at throwaway prices, farmers worry about their livelihoods.
NSE and Jio Platforms embody the sweeping changes in how India has consumed, invested and transacted over the past decade.
Iran missed out on qualifying from the group stage on goal difference after having a stoppage-time winner against Egypt disallowed for a marginal offside.
Morocco score injury-time equaliser before seeing off Netherlands 3-2 on penalties to reach the last 16 of the World Cup.
Germany make a shock last-32 exit against Paraguay, losing a World Cup penalty shootout for the first time to pile pressure on boss Julian Nagelsmann.
Hong Myung-bo has resigned after South Korea failed to advance to the World Cup knockout stages.
Liverpool forward Cody Gakpo scores for the Netherlands days after the loss of his unborn son, as they are knocked out on penalties by Morocco.
Skygazers captured stunning photos of the first full Moon of summer shining bright above the UK
The upcoming defence plan reflects rapid changes in drone warfare, our security correspondent writes.
Dr Cyriac Abby Philips has built a large online following, but often clashes with India's traditional medicine specialists.
After British stars Jack Draper and Emma Raducanu withdrew injured from Wimbledon, BBC Sport analyses why so many top players are struggling with injury.
As a man who has held prime ministerial ambitions for years, Andy Burnham makes his dash for the door, political editor Chris Mason writes.
The US, Canada and Mexico were to reach an agreement on whether to extend their free trade pact by 1 July. All signs point to them blowing past that deadline.
At least 1,700 people are known to have been killed in the disaster but hope remains that others can continue to be found alive.
In areas devastated by the twin earthquakes, people are using crowbars, pickaxes and their bare hands to try to reach survivors.
With tens of thousands of people still believed missing, teams listen for signs of life beneath the rubble.
Teams armed with a range of techniques continue to dig through the rubble in Venezuela.
Pakistan claims it was targeting militants near its border, but the Taliban government calls it an "atrocity".
Ted and his father say it was incredible bumping into Catherine, Princess of Wales.
The Supreme Court decision means the US president will now have to pay Carroll the $5m (£3.6m) in damages she had been awarded.
The app's new usernames feature will be rolled out globally over the next few months.
It comes as regional rivals like Taiwan, China and Japan are investing heavily in chip factories and other technology.
All the latest before the round of 32 games continue at the 2026 Fifa World Cup including France taking on Sweden.
Watch BBC coverage, listen to radio commentary and follow text updates from day two of Wimbledon 2026.
Bruno Guimaraes has been Newcastle United's leader, but Arsenal are lurking with intent and could soon try to prise him away. What happens next, and could he yet stay put?
For Serena Williams, everything and nothing has changed on her return to Wimbledon.
The United States' head of homeland security says he "danced a happy dance" when Iran's elimination from the World Cup was confirmed.
Former England captain Wayne Rooney says he would start Manchester United midfielder Kobbie Mainoo instead of Elliot Anderson in Wednesday's World Cup last-32 tie with DR Congo.
